hook.text: In any meadow, dozens of plant and pollinator species form a web of interactions.

buildup.text: Ecologists map these networks—who visits whom—to understand community stability.

discovery.text: Most networks are 'nested': generalist species interact with many, specialists with few.

twist.text: This nested structure makes networks resilient—losing a specialist hurts, but the web holds.

climax.text: Losing a generalist hub—like bumblebees—can collapse pollination for dozens of plants at once.

punchline.text: Pollination is a network, not a list of pairs.

quiz.question: What makes pollination networks resilient?
options: Nested structure with generalists connecting many species
Having only one pollinator per plant
